Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar May-20-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: RESPONDENTS A STAR HOLDINGS LLC, AND PETER ACWORTH's Motion For Protective Order.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed motion for protective order is granted. All post-judgment discovery against Respondents is stayed while the appeal from the judgment is pending.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar May-14-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar May-13-2025 Continued To May-20-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: RESPONDENTS A STAR HOLDINGS LLC, AND PETER ACWORTH's Motion For Protective Order.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed motion for protective order is continued on the court's own motion to May 20, 2025. Moving parties A Star Holding LLC and Peter Acworth are ordered to provide a supplemental request for judicial notice attaching the supersedeas bond for which judicial notice is sought. The court does not have this document and parties are generally required to provide a copy of documents for which judicial notice is sought with the request for notice. See Local Rule 8.6. Moving parties shall provide the supplemental RJN no later than five court days in advance of the continued hearing date.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Stephen M. Williams In Support Of Motion To Compel Respnses To Special And Form Interrogatories, Set One, To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Set One And For Monetary Sanction
Off Calendar Apr-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER WENDY SHARP's Motion To Compel Responses To Special And Form Interrogatories, Set One, To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Set One And For Monetary Sanction.

Petitioner Wendy Sharp seeks an order compelling responses to collection-related discovery and for sanctions. Respondents oppose on the ground that proceedings in the trial court are stayed because they have perfected their appeal of the judgment and filed a supersedeas appeal bond. The court denies the motion.

This court entered judgment on an arbitration award in favor of petitioner Sharp on January 31, 2025 in the amount of $306,112.86 against respondents Peter Acworth and A Star Holding LLC. Sharp concedes that respondents have filed a supersedeas appeal bond and perfected their appeal, thereby staying enforcement of a judgment or order. Sharp nonetheless contends that the appellate stay does not extend to discovery. This court disagrees where Sharp's discovery is in service of collecting her judgment. In any event an appeal bond serves that function and ensures that Sharp will receive her judgment if it is affirmed. (See CCP 917.1(b).)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Jan-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Stephen M. Williams for Wendy Sharp, Petitioner.
Edward C. Singer for A Star Holdings LLC, Respondent.

Mr. Singer contested the court's tentative decision. After review of the pleadings and hearing oral argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER WENDY SHARP'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION is GRANTED. Final Arbitration Award is confirmed. Petitioner's request for costs and fees associated with filing this action is granted in the reasonable amount of $4,000.

Proposed order and Judgment submitted for review. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
